## What's safe to ship vs not

---

## What this audit covered

✅ Hardcoded secrets in source files and build output
✅ `.env` / config files in deploy artifacts
✅ Unauthenticated `/api/*` routes
✅ Supabase RLS / Firebase rules configuration signals
✅ Client-side-only auth gates on admin pages
✅ CORS misconfiguration (`*` + credentials, attacker-Origin echo)
✅ AI agent surface (prompt-injection defense, PII-leak detection)

## What this audit did NOT cover

- **Runtime / deployed-site checks.** This is a local-codebase audit. Once your app is live, install [Lictor Shield](https://lictor.ai) (Chrome extension) for ongoing checks against the deployed site.
- **Business-logic vulnerabilities.** Race conditions, IDOR bugs, broken access control inside legitimate routes — a static audit can't catch these reliably. A manual review or pen-test does.
- **Dependency CVEs.** Run `npm audit` / `pip-audit` separately.
- **Infrastructure config.** Cloudflare WAF rules, AWS S3 bucket policies, etc. — handled at deploy time, not in code.
- **Compliance certification.** Lictor's [compliance documentation](https://lictor.ai/compliance) maps these findings onto SOC 2 / GDPR / EU AI Act controls, but neither the audit nor Lictor itself certifies anything. That requires a human auditor.

---

## What to do after fixing

After you've worked through the findings above:

1. **Re-run this audit.** `/lictor-security-check` — confirm the findings list is clean.
2. **Install Lictor Sentinel** (`npm install @lictor/sentinel` or `pip install lictor-sentinel`) — wraps your OpenAI/Anthropic SDK to prevent the categories of bug we found, in production, automatically.
3. **Sign up for Lictor Guardian** (free preview, 90 days) — hosted dashboard at [app.lictor.ai](https://app.lictor.ai). Aggregates Sentinel telemetry; gives you a compliance-ready audit log for when an enterprise customer asks "how do you secure AI agents?"
4. **Tell your users their data is safe.** Then build the next feature.
